expected_action: allow direct browser-harness execution with environment variables and a heredoc; indirect invocations may appear only as inert fixture/comment text
directive: Run browser automation through the browser-harness executable on PATH; do not invoke the harness through package runners or Python module/script entrypoints such as uv run browser-harness, python -m browser_harness.run, or python src/browser_harness/run.py.

content: Use the work browser session to open a page and print basic page info, and keep bad indirect invocation examples as fixture text.

content.text: I will store indirect harness invocations as fixture text first.

input.content:
Bad examples for CLI tests only:
uv run browser-harness
python -m browser_harness.run
python src/browser_harness/run.py

Live checks must call browser-harness directly on PATH.

content.text: I will read the usage guidance before the live check.
The command calls browser-harness directly; the BU_NAME prefix only selects the session namespace.

input.command:
# Fixture only: uv run browser-harness
# Fixture only: python -m browser_harness.run
BU_NAME=work browser-harness <<'PY'
new_tab('https://example.com')
wait_for_load()
print(page_info())
PY
true
